about.ABOUT_INTRO_BODY.0: Zakher Travel Şirkətlər Qrupu 2016-cı ildə yaradılıb və qısa müddətə səyahət sənayesində əhəmiyyətli uğurlar əldə edib. İllər ərzində Körfəz regionundan və digər ölkələrdən minlərlə qonağı qəbul etmiş, Azərbaycan, Türkiyə, Polşa, Gürcüstan, Qazaxıstan, Qırğızıstan, Özbəkistan, Rusiya, Çexiya, Ukrayna və BƏƏ daxil olmaqla müxtəlif istiqamətlərdə füsunkar səyahət təcrübələri yaratmışıq.
about.ABOUT_INTRO_BODY.1: Fəaliyyətimizə başladığımız gündən bir neçə ölkədə filiallar açmış, dünya üzrə 2 000-dən çox turizm şirkəti ilə möhkəm tərəfdaşlıq qurmuşuq. 50-dən çox peşəkar işçidən ibarət komandamız hər müştərinin ehtiyacına uyğun fərdi turlar və keyfiyyətli xidmət təqdim edir.
about.ABOUT_INTRO_BODY.2: Tam lisenziyalı tur operatoru kimi beynəlxalq səyyahları seçilmiş istiqamətlərimizə gətiririk. Xüsusilə Qafqazın incisi — Azərbaycanı təbliğ etməyə diqqət yetiririk: qədim tarix, müasir həyat, möhtəşəm təbiət və zəngin mədəniyyət burada bir araya gəlir.
about.ABOUT_INTRO_BODY.3: Dörd fərqli mövsümün hər biri özünəməxsus gözəllik bəxş etdiyi üçün istiqamətlərimiz il boyu ziyarətə açıqdır. Səyyahlar hər səfərdə yeni kəşflər edə bilər.

about.WHO_WE_ARE_BODY.0: Zakher Travel Şirkətlər Qrupu rəsmi olaraq 2016-cı ildə fəaliyyətə başlayıb və qısa müddətdə əhəmiyyətli nailiyyətlər qazanmışdır. QOLF regionundan və digər ölkələrdən minlərlə turist qəbul edərək onları yalnız Azərbaycanda deyil, həm də Türkiyə, Polşa, Gürcüstan, Qazaxıstan, Qırğızıstan, Özbəkistan, Rusiya, Çexiya Respublikası, Ukrayna və BƏƏ kimi ölkələrin cazibədar sərvətləri ilə tanış etməyə başlamışdır. Fəaliyyət illəri ərzində bu ölkələrdə filiallar açmışıq. Biz tur operatoruyuq və 2000-dən çox səyahət şirkəti ilə müqavilələrimiz var. 50 nəfərdən artıq peşəkar heyətimizlə müştərilərimizə fərdi hazırlanmış turlar və peşəkar xidmətlər təqdim edirik.
about.WHO_WE_ARE_BODY.1: Dünyanın müxtəlif yerlərindən turistləri istiqamətlərimizə gətirən az sayda şirkətlərdən biridir. Tarixi və müasir bu ölkəni — Qafqazın incisini — cazibədar təbiəti, tarixi və memarlıq abidələri, zəngin mədəni irsi ilə tanıtmaq üçün əlimizdən gələni edirik.
about.WHO_WE_ARE_BODY.2: Mövsümlərin fərqliliyi və hər mövsümün öz iqlim xüsusiyyətlərinə malik olması sayəsində turistlər istiqamətlərimizi ilin istənilən vaxtı ziyarət edə bilərlər. Hətta ildə bir neçə dəfə gələ bilərsiniz və hər dəfə yeni bir şey kəşf edəcəksiniz.

branches.details.0.description.0: Azərbaycan, often called the “Land of Fire,” is where East meets West and ancient civilizations have left their mark. From the bustling streets of Bakı to historic towns and ancient monuments, the country offers a rich cultural experience.
branches.details.0.description.1: Its landscapes—from the Caspian Sea coastline to high plateaus, mountains, and lush valleys—create a harmony that enchants travelers with heritage, nature, and vibrant culture.
branches.details.0.title: Azərbaycan
branches.details.1.description.0: Often described as a bridge between East and West, Türkiyə is a land where civilizations have met for thousands of years. With a history stretching back over 10,000 years, it has been home to ancient empires whose traces are visible in its cities, monuments, and traditions.
branches.details.1.description.1: Türkiyə is rich in cultural heritage and historical depth. Its diverse landscapes range from Mediterranean and Aegean coastlines to high plateaus, mountains, and fertile valleys, creating a dynamic connection between nature, history, and modern life.
branches.details.1.title: Türkiyə
branches.details.10.description.0: Ukraine, a land of fertile plains, majestic mountains, and vibrant rivers, reflects centuries of culture through its cities and countryside. Kyiv’s golden domes, Lviv’s charming streets, and the Carpathian Mountains highlight its natural beauty.
branches.details.10.description.1: The country is known for resilience, hospitality, and rich folk heritage.
branches.details.10.title: Ukraine
branches.details.2.description.0: Kazakhstan, the heart of the Great Steppes, stretches across vast plains, deserts, serene lakes, and towering mountains. Here, nomadic heritage blends with modern urban life, creating a dynamic and multicultural society.
branches.details.2.description.1: Travelers are fascinated by its breathtaking nature, nomadic exoticism, and a striking modern contrast.
branches.details.2.title: Kazakhstan
branches.details.3.description.0: Often called the land of celestial mountains, Kyrgyzstan is an ancient homeland of nomadic culture with a history rooted in freedom and close connection to nature. The country is dominated by dramatic mountain ranges, alpine meadows, glaciers, and high-altitude lakes.
branches.details.3.description.1: These landscapes have shaped a culture based on mobility, endurance, and harmony with the environment. Kyrgyzstan preserves strong traditions of oral heritage, horsemanship, and communal values.
branches.details.3.title: Kyrgyzstan
branches.details.4.description.0: Uzbekistan, a jewel of the Great Silk Road, boasts cities like Samarkand, Bukhara, and Khiva, where stunning architecture and vibrant arts tell the story of a thriving past.
branches.details.4.description.1: Rich traditions and colorful history make every visit unforgettable. Travelers enjoy its history, art, and ancient culture.
branches.details.4.title: Uzbekistan
branches.details.5.contact.address: str Merab Kostava St, Tbilisi 0179, Gürcüstan
branches.details.5.description.0: Gürcüstan, with a history spanning over 8,000 years, offers landscapes ranging from the peaks of the Qafqaz Mountains to fertile valleys and subtropical coasts. Ancient traditions live alongside modern life, creating a warm and resilient society.
branches.details.5.description.1: Gürcüstan’s identity is deeply tied to its land, language, and historical continuity.
branches.details.5.title: Gürcüstan
branches.details.6.description.0: Located in the heart of Europe, Poland has a long and complex history marked by resilience and cultural continuity. Its landscapes include vast plains, forests, lakes, and a Baltic Sea coastline, as well as mountain ranges in the south.
branches.details.6.description.1: Poland is rich in historical landmarks, intellectual traditions, and cultural heritage.
branches.details.6.title: Poland
branches.details.7.description.0: Known for its medieval cities and rich cultural legacy, the Czech Republic has been a center of art, philosophy, and craftsmanship for centuries. The country’s landscape is characterized by rolling hills, river valleys, forests, and historic towns integrated into the natural environment.
branches.details.7.description.1: This balance between nature and urban heritage contributes to its cultural refinement and architectural harmony.
branches.details.7.title: Czech Republic
branches.details.8.description.0: Spanning Eastern Europe and Northern Asia, Russia is one of the world’s most geographically vast and culturally complex countries. Its landscapes range from tundra and taiga forests to mountains, rivers, steppes, and Arctic coastlines.
branches.details.8.description.1: This immense natural diversity has shaped regional identities and historical development. Russia is known for its cultural depth, literary tradition and astonishing natural landscapes.
branches.details.8.title: Russia
branches.details.9.contact.address: Şarqə International Hava limanı - منطقة حرة - Şarqə - BƏƏ
branches.details.9.description.0: Known as a symbol of rapid development and innovation, the Birləşmiş Ərəb Emirlikləri has transformed from a desert-based society into a global hub within a few decades. Its landscape is defined by vast deserts, coastal areas along the Arabian Gulf, and modern urban centers rising from arid land.
branches.details.9.description.1: Rooted in Bedouin traditions and Islamic values, the BƏƏ reflects a balance between heritage, environment, and technological progress.
branches.details.9.title: Birləşmiş Ərəb Emirlikləri

country.countries.0.description: Azərbaycan, often called the “Land of Fire,” is where East meets West and ancient civilizations have left their mark. From the bustling streets of Bakı to historic towns and ancient monuments, the country offers a rich cultural experience. Its landscapes — from the Caspian Sea coastline to high plateaus, mountains, and lush valleys — create a harmony that enchants travelers with heritage, nature, and vibrant culture.
country.countries.0.faqs.0.answer:
Itinerary: Abşeron – Ateshgah – Yanar Dag – Gala. Götürmə and Endirmə at your hotel within Bakı.
Highlights: Heydar Aliyev Center designed by Zaha Hadid, Ateshgah “Temple of Eternal Fire,” Yanardag — the eternally burning mountain, Gala Archaeological and Ethnographic Museum Complex, Bina Equestrian Center with the famous Karabakh horses, and Heydar Mosque — the biggest mosque in the Qafqaz.
country.countries.0.faqs.0.question: Abşeron Tur — 3 hours, private
country.countries.0.faqs.1.answer:
An independent night tour of Bakı with a private driver and guide.
Highlights: Highland Park with panoramic views of Bakı Bay, National Flag Square, Flame Towers, the Funicular, Carpet Museum, Bakı Boulevard (Seaside Park), Little Venice, Government House, State Philharmonic Hall, and Fountain Square — the most famous street of Bakı.
country.countries.0.faqs.1.question: Bakı Gecə Tur — 2 hours, private
country.countries.0.faqs.10.answer:
Itinerary: Bakı – Shemakha – Lahij – Bakı (approx. 180 km one way, all season, 9–10 hours).
Visit Shemakha, the ancient capital of Shirvan, with Gulistan fortress, the Seven Domes mausoleum, and the 10th-century Juma Mosque. Then explore Lahij — a medieval high-altitude village founded in the 3rd–4th century BC, with cobbled streets, hereditary blacksmiths and coppersmiths, and a 1,500-year-old water system.
country.countries.0.faqs.10.question: Tur to the village of artisans — Lahij, 1 day
country.countries.0.faqs.11.answer: A full-day gastronomic journey: traditional breakfast in the Old City with tandir bread, honey, and cheese; the colorful eastern bazaar with spices, dried fruits, and Caspian black caviar; lunch with sturgeon or lamb kebab and shah-plov at an authentic restaurant; and a stroll through the Old Town with tea or wine tasting.
country.countries.0.faqs.11.question: Tastes and Flavors of Azerbaijani Cuisine — 5 hours
country.countries.0.faqs.12.answer: Contact us at incoming@zakher.travel or +994 12 310 09 32. Our team provides offline support by call, e-mail, and WhatsApp 24/7. Ekonom and VIP transport packages are available on request.
country.countries.0.faqs.12.question: How can I book this tour package?
country.countries.0.faqs.2.answer:
A walking tour through medieval Icheri Sheher.
Highlights: Maiden Tower — the symbol of Bakı, the 12th-century Juma Mosque, the Multani and Bukhara caravanserais, historic bathhouses and khanegahs, Shirvanshah’s Palace (12th–15th century), the Museum of Miniature Books, the Mugham Theatre, Fountain Square, the National History Museum, a sea trip along Bakı Bay, and Bakı’s shopping malls.
country.countries.0.faqs.2.question: Old City Excursion (Walking Tur) — 2.5 hours
country.countries.0.faqs.3.answer:
Itinerary: Bakı – Qobustan – Mud Volcanoes – Bakı (approx. 65 km one way, all season, Cip 4×4).
Visit the Qobustan Museum of Petroglyphs — prehistoric rock drawings carved by primitive men in the Beyukdash, Kichikdash, Djingirdag and Shighgaya mountains — then trip to the mud volcanoes: about 350 of the world’s 800 mud volcanoes are found in Azərbaycan, most of them in Qobustan.
country.countries.0.faqs.3.question: Museum Qobustan and Mud Volcanoes Tur — 5 hours
country.countries.0.faqs.4.answer:
Itinerary: Bakı – Alat – Volcano Dashgil – Kichik Dash – Boyukdash – ascent to the Kyanizadag volcano – Jingirdag – Bakı (approx. 70 km one way, March–October, Cip 4×4).
Climb to the very crater of active mud volcanoes of the Dashgil group and watch the gurgling liquid clay — scenery so unusual that scientists compare it to the surface of Mars.
country.countries.0.faqs.4.question: Extreme Cip Safari — Qobustan Reserve, 5–9 hours
country.countries.0.faqs.5.answer:
Itinerary: Bakı – Shamakhi – Gabala – Bakı (approx. 220 km one way, all season, 10–11 hours).
Gabala overlooks Bazardyuzyu, the highest peak of Azərbaycan (4,466 m). Visit the ruins of ancient Kabalaka — for 600 years the capital of Caucasian Albania — the ancient village of Nij with its Albanian temple, Tufandag summer–winter complex and ropeway, Nohur Lake, and the “Seven Beauties” waterfall.
country.countries.0.faqs.5.question: Tur to Gabala — the capital of Caucasian Albania, 1 day
country.countries.0.faqs.6.answer:
Itinerary: Bakı – Quba – Xınalıq – Bakı (approx. 220 km one way, all season, 10–12 hours).
Xınalıq is the oldest settlement in Azərbaycan, over 5,000 years old, whose residents consider themselves descendants of the biblical Noah. On the way explore Quba — famous for its apples, carpet weaving, the Gechresh forest, Tangaalti gorge, Afurdja waterfall, and the Red Village, home to one of the world’s largest communities of Mountain Jews.
country.countries.0.faqs.6.question: Tur to Xınalıq — 2,200 meters above sea level, 1 day
country.countries.0.faqs.7.answer:
Itinerary: Bakı – Shemakha – Ismayilli – Gabala – Oguz – Şəki – Bakı (approx. 320 km one way, all season, 12–13 hours).
Şəki is one of the oldest settlements in the Qafqaz with findings dating back more than 2,500 years. See the majestic Şəki Khan’s Palace built without a single nail, the ancient Albanian temple in Kish, Gelersen-Gerersen fortress, caravanserais, Juma Mosque, and medieval baths. Optional stops include the Diri Baba Mausoleum and Nohur Lake.
country.countries.0.faqs.7.question: Tur to Şəki — Ancient Khanate, 1 day
country.countries.0.faqs.8.answer:
Itinerary: Bakı – Gusar – Şahdağ – Laza – Bakı (approx. 220 km one way, 10–12 hours).
09:30 departure from Bakı with a stop near Beshbarmag Mountain; arched bridge across Gudiyalchay river and the Red Village on the way. Arrival at the Şahdağ summer–winter tourism complex — Azərbaycan’s first ski resort — then a trip to Laza village, an exotic area with majestic waterfalls and horse-riding opportunities.
country.countries.0.faqs.8.question: “The North Gates” Tur — Gusar + Şahdağ + Laza, 1 day
country.countries.0.faqs.9.answer:
Itinerary: Bakı – Quba – Bakı (approx. 220 km one way, all season, 10–12 hours).
Highlights: Quba fruit gardens, the fabled Xınalıq village, Chanlibel Lake, Quba Genocide Memorial Complex, Qechresh Forest, “Nazli Bulag” spring, “Mest Dergah” waterfall, Red Town, the Quba carpet workshop, Azərbaycan National Qolf Club, Quba bazaar and sweet factory, and the Şahdağ Tourism Complex with cable car rides and skiing.
country.countries.0.faqs.9.question: Tur to the North — Forests and Mountains of Quba, 1 day

country.countries.9.description: Located in the heart of Europe, Poland has a long and complex history marked by resilience and cultural continuity. Its landscapes include vast plains, forests, lakes, and a Baltic Sea coastline, as well as mountain ranges in the south. Poland is rich in historical landmarks, intellectual traditions, and cultural heritage.
country.countries.9.faqs.0.answer: Walking tour of the old town: St. Florian’s Gate, Wawel Hill with the Royal Castle and Cathedral, the Main Market Square, Kazimierz with New Square and Szeroka Street, St. Mary’s Basilica, handmade souvenir and antique shops, the Jagiellonian University district, Planty Park, and Energylandia. Optional sightseeing by electric golf car covering the Cloth Hall, Town Hall, Barbican, the synagogues of Kazimierz, Ghetto Heroes Square, and Oskar Schindler’s Factory.
country.countries.9.faqs.0.question: Krakow City Tur
country.countries.9.faqs.1.answer: Gubalowka funicular, the “Wielka Krokiew” ski jump, Krupowki Street, Willa Koliba — the Museum of Zakopane Style, Koscieliska street and valley with optional horse-and-carriage ride, Kalatowki Valley, the Regional Museum, thermal pools, and traditional mountaineers’ cottages.
country.countries.9.faqs.1.question: Zakopane Tur
country.countries.9.faqs.2.answer: Wieliczka Salt Mine — one of the first 12 UNESCO World Heritage Sites in the world: a 2-hour route through winding corridors, 800 steps, and a descent to 135 meters underground, with statues and chandeliers carved directly in salt rock. Bochnia Salt Mine: underground vaults with a railway ride, the world’s longest underground slide, a ferry on the brine lake, and the Chapel of Saint Kinga at 212 meters underground.
country.countries.9.faqs.2.question: Wieliczka & Bochnia Salt Mines
country.countries.9.faqs.3.answer: Visit the largest concentration camp of the Nazi Holocaust with a professional guide: the infamous “Arbeit Macht Frei” gate, the barracks and gas chambers, both Auschwitz I and Auschwitz II-Birkenau, and the wailing wall where visitors pay their respects to the victims.
country.countries.9.faqs.3.question: Auschwitz-Birkenau Memorial and Museum
country.countries.9.faqs.4.answer: Trace the story of Krakow’s Jewish community through the centuries: explore Kazimierz with its synagogues, monuments and kosher restaurants, visit the Old Synagogue and Remuh Synagogue and Cemetery, hear about the WWII ghetto, see locations where Steven Spielberg filmed Schindler’s List, and visit the Oskar Schindler’s Factory museum.

tours.byCountry.azerbaijan.0.excerpt: Qolf holidays at The National Azərbaycan Qolf Club and Dreamland Qolf Club, with special packages from 500 USD.
The National Azərbaycan Qolf Club is located in the area of the Quba Palace Otel in the north-west of Azərbaycan, sitting at the foot of the breathtaking Qafqaz Mountains. Designed by Jon Hunt from International Qolf Design, this was the first golf course to open in the “land of fire.” During the summer of 2014, less than two months after its official unveiling, it hosted the country’s inaugural professional event — the Azərbaycan Challenge Open.
A fully equipped clubhouse offers luxurious locker rooms, a professional shop, and a truly unique dining experience. Positioned within 75 hectares of idyllic Azerbaijani topography, the undulating fairways and deep bunkers create a lasting impression, while accessible landing zones and greens give a “look hard, play easy” feel. Distance from Bakı to Quba: 168 km.
At Dreamland you will find everything about the Dye Course — from green fees and hire charges to hole-by-hole flyover videos. The luxury clubhouse is a place to eat, drink and socialize, with a golf shop, dress code, and the hole-in-one Roll of Honour. Dreamland is an IMG Qolf club. Distance from Bakı: 22 km.
Quba Palace Otel 5*, Deluxe King room with mountain view.
Daxildir: 3 nights accommodation in Quba, Bufet səhər yeməyi, 3 rounds of golf at The National Qolf Club Quba, private car with driver, golf car, Quba Şəhər turu, Şahdağ or Xınalıq Village tour, Bütün transferlər (Bakı–Quba–Bakı), and Bütün vergilər.
Special Qolf Paket — 3 nights in Quba (from 540 USD per pax)
Accommodation: Fairmont Otel 5* (Deluxe King City View), Dreamland Otel 4*, or Mercure Bakı City Otel 5* (from 500 USD per pax).
Program: Gün 1 — transfer to hotel; Gün 2 — Bakı Şəhər turu; Gün 3 — 1 round of golf at Dreamland Qolf Club and Abşeron tour; Gün 4 — transfer to airport.
Daxildir: 3 nights accommodation, Bufet səhər yeməyi, 1 round of golf, private car with driver, golf car, Bakı Şəhər turu, Abşeron tour, Bütün transferlər and taxes.
Daxil deyil: Aviabiletlər, visa, meals and drinks, personal expenditures, guide.
Special Qolf Paket — 3 nights in Bakı (from 800 USD for 2 pax)
Accommodation: Quba Palace Otel 5* and Fairmont Otel 5* (or Dreamland Otel 4* / Mercure Bakı City Otel 5*).
Program: Gün 1 — transfer and 1 round of golf at The National Qolf Club Quba; Gün 2 — golf and Quba Şəhər turu; Gün 3 — golf and Şahdağ or Xınalıq Village tour; Gün 4 — return to Bakı; Gün 5 — Bakı Şəhər turu; Gün 6 — 1 round of golf at Dreamland Qolf Club; Gün 7 — Abşeron tour; Gün 8 — transfer to airport.
Daxildir: 3 nights in Quba + 4 nights in Bakı, Bufet səhər yeməyi, 4 rounds of golf (3 in Quba, 1 in Bakı), city tours, private car with driver, golf car, Bütün transferlər and taxes.
Daxil deyil: Aviabiletlər, visa, meals and drinks, personal expenditures, guide.
Special Qolf Paket — 7 nights in Azərbaycan (from 1600 USD for 2 pax)
For reservation please contact us: info@zakher.travel, +994 50 253 22 00.

Bakı daxilində otelinizdən götürmə və endirmə
tours.byCountry.azerbaijan.1.packages.0: Ekonom paket: peşəkar sürücü, turlar zamanı fərdi rahat nəqliyyat (Mercedes Viano/Vito) və bələdçi xidməti.
tours.byCountry.azerbaijan.1.packages.1: VIP paket: geniş lüks avtomobil seçimi (Mercedes S, E, V sinif və s.).
09:30 — departure from Bakı to Gusar with a stop near Beshbarmag Mountain. On the way you will see the arched bridge across the Gudiyalchay river and the Red Village in Quba district, home to one of the largest communities of Mountain Jews in the world.
13:00–14:00 — dinner at a local restaurant (for extra payment).
15:00 — arrival at the summer & winter touristic complex Şahdağ.
16:00 — trip to Laza, an exotic area with a large number of waterfalls and an opportunity for horse riding.
17:00–20:00 — departure to Bakı.
Itinerary: Bakı – Gusar – Şahdağ – Laza – Bakı
Gusar is the last major settlement in the north of Azərbaycan, called “The North Gates” as it borders the Republic of Dagestan. The population consists of Lezgins — an original nationality with a rich culture known for hospitality. Four of the nine climatic zones are found in Gusar. The region is famous for the Şahdağ summer–winter complex, operating all year round: a popular ski resort in winter and a camp site on the slopes of the Greater Qafqaz in summer. Part of the territory belongs to Şahdağ National Park — the largest in Azərbaycan. Historical monuments include the fortress walls in Enih village (13th century), the 1544 tomb of Sheikh Juneyd, and the 18th-century mosques of Kohne Khudat and Khuray. The folklore ensemble “Lezginka” keeps alive the famous Caucasian dance of the same name.
Laza village lies on the north-east outskirts of the Main Caucasian Ridge, at the foot of the “Shah Yaylag” plateau and the 4,242 m Şahdağ peak, at an altitude of 1,300 m on an ancient caravan route. Its population consists mostly of ethnic Lezgins whose history is closely connected with Caucasian Albania, one of the most ancient states on the territory of Azərbaycan. In winter most of the waterfalls freeze and climbing competitions are organized on the frozen falls.

tours.byCountry.azerbaijan.10.sections.0: Understanding the development of medieval Icheri Sheher and urban life in Bakı is key to understanding the current economic, material, and moral culture of present-day Azərbaycan.
tours.byCountry.azerbaijan.10.sections.1:
Maiden Tower — the symbol of Bakı in the southeastern part of Icheri Sheher.
Juma Mosque (12th century) — a center of the social, political, and cultural life of medieval Azərbaycan.
Old caravanserais — the 14th-century Multani Caravanserai on Qulle Street and the Bukhara Caravanserai opposite, shelters for merchants on the caravan routes.
Bathhouses — half-underground hammams, warm in winter and cool in summer.
Khanegahs — dwellings of the Sufis along the trade routes.
Shirvanshah’s Palace (12th–15th century) — the last residence of the rulers of Shirvan, built after the capital moved to Bakı following the 1191 earthquake.
Museum of Miniature Books — 4,350 miniature books from 62 countries on display.
The Mugham Theatre — a 15th-century two-floored caravanserai with an octagonal courtyard.
Fountain Square, the National History Museum, a sea trip along Bakı Bay, and Bakı’s largest shopping malls.

tours.byCountry.azerbaijan.11.sections.0: Spend a day like a real Bakı citizen and try the best dishes in authentic restaurants. This excursion opens the world of the richest Azerbaijani cuisine: a gastronomic journey through the Old City, the colorful Bakı bazaar, and the coast of the Caspian Sea. In one day you will learn new shades of flavors and try local delicacies — the most delicious lamb and sturgeon, fragrant wines, countless spices, and dried fruits.
tours.byCountry.azerbaijan.11.sections.1:
Səhər yeməyi in the Old Town — rustic butter, cheese, honey, hot cakes freshly baked in tandir, tea or coffee.
The eastern bazaar — one of the oldest Bakı bazaars: the specifics of Azerbaijani cuisine, its main ingredients and spices; taste and buy spices, dried fruits, fresh Caspian black caviar, and exclusive Azerbaijani teas.
Lunch — shish kebab from freshly caught sturgeon or lamb, shah-plov, and other traditional dishes at a traditional restaurant.
Authentic dishes and secret recipes — a stroll through the iconic sights of the Old Town with tea or wine tasting.
Organizational details — food and drinks are paid separately; transportation is included. Tell us your culinary preferences in advance.
tours.byCountry.azerbaijan.11.title: Tastes and Flavors of Azerbaijani Cuisine

tours.byCountry.azerbaijan.12.sections.0: Gabala is a unique and beautiful region on the bank of the Demiraparanchay River, overlooking the highest peak of Azərbaycan — Bazardyuzyu (4,466 m). The main sight is the ruins of an ancient town 15 km from the city center: “Kabalaka,” mentioned in 1st-century historical sources and for 600 years the capital of Caucasian Albania.
tours.byCountry.azerbaijan.12.sections.1: The ancient village of Nij with its Albanian temple (4th–7th centuries) — homeland of the Azerbaijani-Udi ethnic group, descendants of the Caucasian Albanians; the ancient defensive tower (9th–11th centuries); and Ustajan Tower (9th–14th centuries). Modern hotel and resort infrastructure serves the most demanding travelers, and every summer Gabala hosts international music festivals.
tours.byCountry.azerbaijan.12.sections.2: The ancient town of Kabalaka, Gabala Land entertainment center, the Tufandag summer–winter touristic complex, the ropeway, Nij village and its Albanian temple, Gabala Shooting Center, Nohur Lake, and the “Seven Beauties” waterfall.

tours.byCountry.azerbaijan.13.sections.0: Xınalıq is the oldest settlement in Azərbaycan, on the northern slope of the Main Caucasian Ridge at an altitude of 2,100–2,200 m. Its residents consider themselves direct descendants of the biblical Noah, and the village’s history goes back more than 5,000 years. For centuries it was cut off from civilization by hard-to-reach mountains with dangerous rocky cliffs.
tours.byCountry.azerbaijan.13.sections.1: Today there are about 380 houses, 200–300 years old, built so closely on the steep slopes that the roof of one house serves as the patio of the one above. The inhabitants have retained their traditional way of life, culture, and original language.
tours.byCountry.azerbaijan.13.sections.2: Quba district is a favorite destination 168 km from Bakı: the shady Gechresh forest, Tangaalti gorge, the Velvelichay river, and the famous Afurdja waterfall, listed among the “Nature Monuments of Azərbaycan.” The Red Village here is home to one of the largest communities of Mountain Jews in the world. Quba is famous for its apples and is a recognized carpet-weaving center; it was visited by Alexandre Dumas, Bestuzhev-Marlinsky, and Thor Heyerdahl.

tours.byCountry.azerbaijan.14.sections.1: Famous for apples and carpet-making, Quba is one of the major and oldest cities of Azərbaycan, 165 km north of Bakı, with unique nature, a pleasant climate, and gorgeous air. It is a popular getaway and the gateway to Azərbaycan’s most interesting remote mountain villages.
tours.byCountry.azerbaijan.14.sections.2: Quba fruit gardens fed by crystal-clear melt water; presentation of the national sweet “Bukme”; the fabled village of Xınalıq; Chanlibel Lake — one of the most beautiful lakes of Azərbaycan; the Quba Genocide Memorial Complex; Qechresh Forest with its natural springs; the “Nazli Bulag” spring; the “Mest Dergah” waterfall with wooden cottages; the Red Town — primary settlement of Mountain Jews (about 4,000 people); the Quba carpet workshop; Azərbaycan National Qolf Club — the first golf course of the “land of fire”; Quba bazaar; the Quba sweet factory; and the Şahdağ Tourism Complex with cable car rides and skiing.

tours.byCountry.azerbaijan.15.sections.0: Shemakha is an ancient capital of Azərbaycan, founded in the 5th century BC. In the 9th–16th centuries it was the capital of the Shirvan khanate, residence of the Shirvanshahs, and one of the most beautiful cities of the East. The main sight is the 11th–12th-century Gulistan fortress; also the Seven Domes (Yeddi Gumbez) mausoleum, the 10th-century Juma Mosque — the oldest in the Qafqaz after Derbent — and ruins from the 10th–17th centuries. Today Shemakha is a winemaking and carpet-weaving center and the hometown of many Azerbaijani poets.
tours.byCountry.azerbaijan.15.sections.1: The high-altitude village of Lahij in Ismayilli region is an original monument of ancient architecture, founded in the 3rd–4th century BC. In this medieval town, famous far beyond the Qafqaz, cobbled streets and ancient squares are preserved in their original form. Here you will find copper, leather, and wood articles, peculiar samples of cold steel, and many workshops where hereditary blacksmiths still create household items — you can watch the process and buy freshly made goods. Remarkably, the water and sewage system was built more than 1,500 years ago, and no one knows where it starts, where it ends, or how it works.
tours.byCountry.azerbaijan.15.title: Tur to the Village of Artisans — Lahij

Museum of Petroglyphs (outdoor museum) — Qobustan is located approximately 65 km from Bakı. Prehistoric rock drawings — petroglyphs — are an art “archive” of human evolution on Earth. The largest such outdoor archive in Azərbaycan is in Qobustan, at the Bakı State Reserve of History, Ethnography and Arts, near the Caspian Sea on the ancient Shirvan road. In the mountains of Beyukdash, Kichikdash, Djingirdag and Shighgaya are concentrated rock paintings, ancient camps, and tombstones from the Stone Age onward, reflecting the culture, economy, worldview, customs, and traditions of the ancient peoples of Azərbaycan.
Itinerary: Bakı – Sangachal – Kyanizadag volcano – Boyukdash – Bakı
By the number of mud volcanoes Azərbaycan takes first place in the world: approximately 350 of the 800 existing mud volcanoes are found here, most of them concentrated in the Qobustan region.

Şəki is an ancient historical city in the southern foothills of the Greater Qafqaz, 300 km north-west of Bakı. Archaeological evidence suggests Şəki is one of the oldest settlements in the Qafqaz, with findings dating back more than 2,500 years.
Itinerary: Bakı – Shemakha – Ismayilli – Gabala – Oguz – Şəki – Bakı
Many historical monuments are preserved in their original form. The main sight is the majestic Şəki Khan’s Palace with its magnificent wall paintings, constructed in the 18th century without using a single nail. Other popular places: the ancient Albanian temple in Kish settlement, Gelersen-Gerersen fortress (8th–9th centuries), numerous caravanserais, the house of Şəki Khans, Juma Mosque (18th century), the minaret of the Gileili mosque, medieval baths, the theater, the historical museum, and the house-museum of writer M. F. Akhundov.
Diri Baba Mausoleum, Juma Mosque in Shemakha, the ancient city of Kabalaka, the Tufandag summer–winter complex, Nij village with its ancient Albanian temple, Nohur Lake, the Palace of Şəki Khans, and the caravanserai.

tours.byCountry.kazakhstan.0.excerpt: 7-day program: Big Almaty Lake, Shymbulak, Medeu, Kok-Tobe, and Issyk Lake.
Transfer from Almaty International Hava limanı to the hotel and rest. Almaty Şəhər turu: Arbat Street (downtown), President Park, and a national night with dinner at the Alasha restaurant.
Ile-Alatau National Park, Alma-Arasan village, the Big Almatinka–Prokhodnaya river and spring, Zailiyskiy Alatau Mountain, Big Almaty Lake, lunch at Alpen Rose Resort, and a birds show (starting 17:00).
Shymbulak ski complex by gondola road (cable car), the Medeu high-altitude ice rink — an outdoor speed skating and bandy rink — and Dostyk Mall.
Issyk Lake and boat tours, lunch at Stetson Rancho Resort (horse center), the river, and the Turgen (Ayuly) waterfalls.
Kok-Tobe entertainment park (cable car and fast coaster), Central Park, the Dolphinarium, and the Zoo Park.
Alış-veriş turu: Mega Mall, Esentai Mall, Barakholka shopping, a local chocolate boutique, and the Green Bazaar.
Transfer from the hotel to the airport.

tours.byCountry.kyrgyzstan.0.excerpt: 6-day program: Burana Tower, Ala-Archa park, Chunkurchak valley, and hot springs.
Panfilov Entertainment Park, Duboviy (Oak) Park, Central Ala-Too Square, and the Pobeda (Victory) Monument.
Burana Tower and the stone balbals, Kegety Valley, and the Kegety waterfalls.
Ala-Archa Gorge National Park and the Alamedin River (perfect for a picnic by the water). Acquaintance with the traditional ethno complex Supara: everything traditionally Kyrgyz, with a full range of authentic Kyrgyz meals served in comfortably furnished yurts and wooden patio seating.
Chunkurchak Valley, the Chunkurchak ski area, and the ethno resort “Supara Chunkurchak” — suitable for resting, picnics in the valley, short hikes, and breathing ozonized mountain air.
Alamedin waterfalls, the “Teplie Klyuchi” hot springs, and the Alamedin River.
Asia Mall, Ala-Archa Trade Centre, Bishkek Park, and the honey exhibition fair.

tours.byCountry.poland.2.excerpt: UNESCO underground world: salt chapels, brine lakes, and 135 m descents.
tours.byCountry.poland.2.meta.0.label: Sites
tours.byCountry.poland.2.meta.1.label: Tur length
tours.byCountry.poland.2.sections.0.body: The Wieliczka Salt Mine was one of the first 12 sites in the world designated a UNESCO World Heritage Site and is one of the most visited tourist sites anywhere. The 2-hour tour covers only 1% of the underground world: winding corridors almost 3 km long, 800 steps, and a descent to a depth of 135 meters through the Danilowicz Shaft. Explore a vast labyrinth of tunnels and chambers and admire intricate statues, chandeliers, and other art carved directly in the salt rock.
tours.byCountry.poland.2.sections.0.head: Wieliczka Salt Mine
tours.byCountry.poland.2.sections.1.body: Hear the story of Duke Boleslaw V the Chaste and Duchess Kinga, ride the underground railway, go down the world’s longest underground slide, and sail a ferry on the brine lake flooding the chamber. Visit a network of chambers with unusual geological structures, a stable, an exhibition of wooden tread-wheels, and the Chapel of Saint Kinga at a depth of 212 meters underground.

tours.byCountry.poland.3.meta.0.label: Site
tours.byCountry.poland.3.meta.1.label: Kateqoriya
tours.byCountry.poland.3.sections.0.body: Visit the Auschwitz-Birkenau complex and learn about its terrible history from a professional guide. See the infamous “Arbeit Macht Frei” entrance gate, the barracks where prisoners were kept, and the gas chambers. The tour covers both parts of the camp — Auschwitz I and Auschwitz II-Birkenau — and ends at the wailing wall, where visitors pay their respects to the victims of the Nazi Holocaust.

tours.byCountry.poland.4.excerpt: 4-hour Jewish history and heritage tour of Krakow, including Schindler's Factory.
tours.byCountry.poland.4.meta.0.label: Müddət
tours.byCountry.poland.4.meta.1.label: District
tours.byCountry.poland.4.sections.0.body: Trace the story of Krakow’s Jewish community through the centuries and during WWII. Explore Kazimierz — the city’s historical Jewish district — with its synagogues, monuments, and kosher restaurants. Visit the Old Synagogue, Remuh Synagogue and Cemetery, hear how the people of Kazimierz were herded into the Jewish ghetto, learn how Steven Spielberg filmed Schindler’s List here, and visit the Oskar Schindler’s Factory museum to see how Schindler saved many Jewish lives.

tours.byCountry.ukraine.0.sections.0.bod: A walk to Poshtova Ploshcha (the Dnieper River station) and a ride on the Kyiv Metro to the deepest metro station in the world — Arsenalna (105.5 m below the surface). The Motherland Monument (102 m), the Museum of the History of Ukraine in WWII, the Park of Eternal Glory, Kyiv Pechersk Lavra, Khreshchatyk — the main street of Kyiv — the Kyiv Passage, Independence Square (Maidan Nezalezhnosti), Sofia’s Square, the Kyiv funicular (Saint Volodymyr Hill), Andrew’s Descent, and the Landscape Alley.
tours.byCountry.ukraine.0.sections.1.bod: Shchekavytsia mountain, Heydar Aliyev Square, Khreshchaty Park with the People’s Friendship Arch, the Golden Gate, and the National Botanical Garden.
tours.byCountry.ukraine.0.sections.2.bod: Globus Mall, Ocean Plaza, Gulliver Mall, the Bessarabian market, and Lavina Mall.
tours.byCountry.ukraine.0.sections.3.bod:
Gün 4: the Museum of Folk Architecture and Folkways of Ukraine, Feofaniya Park, and Mezhyhirya National Park (the former residence of the fourth President of Ukraine).
Gün 5: the “12 Months” contact zoo, Pushcha-Vodytsia Park, and Koncha-Zaspa Park.
tours.byCountry.ukraine.0.sections.4.bod:
Gün 6: the Uman district.
Gün 7: Vysokyi Verkh mountain with its cable car, Synevyr Lake — the largest lake in the Ukrainian Carpathians — and the resort of Bukovel, the largest ski resort in Eastern Europe.
tours.byCountry.ukraine.0.sections.5.bod: The Lviv Theatre of Opera and Ballet; Market Square with its 45 old architectural buildings and 4 fountains — the Town Hall, the Black House, the Venetian House (Chocolate Museum), and the Pharmacy Museum; High Castle Hill; and the Vernissage market.

whyChooseUs.WHY_CHOOSE_US_CARDS.2.descri: Onlayn ödənişlər internet vasitəsilə mallar və ya xidmətlər üçün — onlayn və ya oflayn alınmış olsun — aparılan əməliyyatlardır. Kredit/debet kartı əməliyyatları, rəqəmsal pul kisələri və bank köçürmələri daxil olmaqla təhlükəsiz platformalardan istifadə edir. Bu ödənişlər e-ticarət və onlayn bankçılıq üçün vacibdir.

whyChooseUs.WHY_CHOOSE_US_CARDS.3.descri: Dünya səviyyəli xidmət müştəri gözləntilərini davamlı aşmaq və bütün qarşılıqlı əlaqələrdə füsunkar təcrübələr təqdim etmək öhdəliyidir. Fərdi, proaktiv və yaddaqalan münasibətlərə fokuslanmaqla adi müştəri xidmətindən kənara çıxır. Bu, müştəri ehtiyaclarını anlamaq, sorğularını əvvəlcədən görmək və məmnuniyyət və sədaqət yaratmaq üçün əlavə səylər göstərməyi əhatə edir.
